message:
test(kernel_reference): fragmented-extent golden via debugfs blocks

Adds a second reference-image scenario to kernel_reference.rs that
constructs a deterministic fragmented two-extent file on ext4 and
differentially compares FrankenFS's `collect_extents` output against
`debugfs blocks` on the same file. Closes the final gap in the
"ext4 extent entry decode" parity row.

crates/ffs-harness/tests/kernel_reference.rs
  * Docstring updated: "Extent mapping: collect_extents matches
    debugfs blocks, including a deterministic fragmented two-extent
    file" (was previously only asserting non-empty results).
  * New helper `create_fragmented_extent_reference_image(image_path)`:
      - Creates a 32 MiB ext4 image via mkfs.ext4 with deterministic
        geometry (4 KiB block size, fixed UUID, disabled resize_inode
        so block allocation is reproducible).
      - Uses debugfs -w to allocate two disjoint extents on a single
        file: first extent at a low logical range, second at a higher
        range with a hole between them. The gap is chosen so the
        parser sees a canonical two-extent `ee_` tree rather than a
        single coalesced extent.
  * New assertion block compares `reader.collect_extents(inode)` to
    the block list returned by `debugfs -R "blocks /fragmented"`:
    same count, same order, same (logical, physical, length) triples.
  * Imports reordered to alphabetical per the previous rustfmt
    commit: `Ext4ImageReader` now sorted to its canonical position
    in the `ffs_ondisk::{ … }` import block.

No fuzz or runtime-code change in this commit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
